yaml.title: Practical Guide & Decision Framework
yaml.subtitle: Decision flowchart, checklist, common mistakes, dan case study end-to-end

markdown.markdown:
```{r} #| label: setup #| include: false source("_common.R") pop <- load_population() N <- nrow(pop) ``` Bab ini menyatukan seluruh materi menjadi panduan praktis yang dapat langsung diterapkan di lapangan. Gunakan flowchart berikut untuk menentukan metode sampling yang tepat: ```{mermaid} %%| fig-width: 10 %%| label: fig-decision-flowchart flowchart TD START["🎯 Tujuan: Estimasi parameter
populasi?"] -->|Ya| FRAME{"Sampling frame
tersedia?"} START -->|Tidak, hanya eksplorasi| NP["Non-probability
sampling OK"] FRAME -->|Ya, lengkap| BUDGET{"Budget &
logistik OK
untuk SRS?"} FRAME -->|Tidak lengkap| HIDDEN{"Hidden /
hard-to-reach
population?"} FRAME -->|Parsial| ADJUST["Perbaiki frame +
model-based
adjustment"] HIDDEN -->|Ya| SNOW["Snowball / RDS
+ network-size
weighting"] HIDDEN -->|Tidak| QUOTA["Quota sampling +
calibration
weighting"] BUDGET -->|Ya| HETERO{"Populasi
heterogen
antar group?"} BUDGET -->|Tidak, terlalu mahal| GEO{"Constraint
geografis?"} HETERO -->|Ya, stratifying var ada| STRAT["✅ Stratified
Random Sampling"] HETERO -->|Tidak, cukup homogen| SRS["✅ Simple Random
Sampling"] GEO -->|Ya| CLUSTER{"Cluster size
bervariasi?"} GEO -->|Tidak| STRAT CLUSTER -->|Ya, sangat bervariasi| PPS["✅ PPS Cluster
Sampling"] CLUSTER -->|Tidak, relatif sama| CLUST_SRS["✅ Cluster +
SRS dalam cluster"] STRAT --> SUBGROUP{"Perlu analisis
subgroup?"} SRS --> SUBGROUP PPS --> SUBGROUP CLUST_SRS --> SUBGROUP SUBGROUP -->|Ya| INFLATE["Inflate n per
subgroup +
DEFF adjustment"] SUBGROUP -->|Tidak| CALC["Hitung n
(Chapter 5)"] INFLATE --> CALC style START fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb,stroke-width:2px style STRAT fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669,stroke-width:2px style SRS fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669,stroke-width:2px style PPS fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669,stroke-width:2px style CLUST_SRS fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669,stroke-width:2px style NP fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706,stroke-width:2px style SNOW fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706,stroke-width:2px style QUOTA fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706,stroke-width:2px ``` ## Checklist Sebelum Sampling ::: {.callout-tip} ## Pre-Sampling Checklist ### 1. Sampling Frame - [ ] Apakah frame tersedia dan up-to-date? - [ ] Berapa coverage rate terhadap target population? - [ ] Apakah ada duplikasi, undercoverage, atau overcoverage? - [ ] Kapan terakhir frame diupdate? ### 2. Stratifikasi - [ ] Variabel stratifikasi apa yang tersedia di frame? - [ ] Apakah variabel tersebut berkorelasi dengan variabel survei utama? - [ ] Berapa strata optimal? (jangan terlalu banyak — minimum n per stratum ≥ 20) ### 3. Budget & Logistik - [ ] Berapa total budget untuk fieldwork? - [ ] Berapa cost per interview (termasuk transportasi, honorarium, supervisi)? - [ ] Berapa banyak interviewer tersedia dan terlatih? - [ ] Berapa lama periode fieldwork? ### 4. Subgroup Analysis - [ ] Subgroup mana yang harus dianalisis secara terpisah? - [ ] Apakah perlu estimasi per subgroup atau hanya overall? - [ ] Berapa presisi minimum per subgroup? ### 5. Non-Response - [ ] Berapa expected response rate (dari survei sebelumnya)? - [ ] Apakah ada strategi follow-up untuk non-respondent? - [ ] Data apa yang tersedia untuk non-response adjustment? ### 6. Quality Control - [ ] Bagaimana verifikasi data di lapangan? - [ ] Berapa persen back-checking yang dilakukan? - [ ] Apakah ada real-time monitoring dashboard? ::: ## 10 Kesalahan Paling Sering ### 1. Mengabaikan Design Effect ::: {.callout-important} **Masalah:** Menghitung sample size dengan formula SRS, lalu menggunakan cluster sampling di lapangan. **Dampak:** Presisi aktual jauh lebih rendah dari yang diharapkan. Jika DEFF = 3, presisi Anda setara dengan n/3. **Solusi:** Selalu kalikan n_SRS dengan DEFF. Jika ICC tidak diketahui, gunakan DEFF = 2.0 sebagai default konservatif. ::: ### 2. Mengabaikan Non-Response **Masalah:** Menargetkan n = 400 tapi response rate hanya 60% → effective n = 240. **Solusi:** $n_{final} = n_{target} / (1 - r)$. Budget untuk follow-up. ### 3. Menganalisis Tanpa Weight **Masalah:** Menggunakan `mean(data$y)` alih-alih `svymean(~y, design)`. **Dampak:** Biased point estimates dan underestimated standard errors. ### 4. Melaporkan Unweighted Counts **Masalah:** "Tabel menunjukkan 45% responden tinggal di urban." Ini unweighted proportion — bukan estimasi populasi. ### 5. Subset Data Sebelum svydesign() **Masalah:** Memfilter data untuk subgroup analisis sebelum mendefinisikan survey design → SE underestimated. **Solusi:** Gunakan `subset()` pada design object. ### 6. Memperlakukan Cluster Effect Sebagai Fixed **Masalah:** Menggunakan fixed effects untuk cluster ID alih-alih memperlakukannya sebagai random effect / PSU dalam survey design. ### 7. Tidak Memeriksa Kualitas Frame **Masalah:** Langsung sampling dari frame tanpa assessment → coverage error tidak terdeteksi. ### 8. Over-Stratification **Masalah:** Terlalu banyak strata → beberapa strata memiliki n < 10 → estimasi tidak stabil. **Rule of thumb:** Minimum 2 PSU per stratum untuk estimasi varians. ### 9. Mengabaikan Multi-Objective **Masalah:** Menghitung n hanya untuk satu variabel, padahal survei mengukur 20 variabel → beberapa variabel underpowered. ### 10. Tidak Melakukan Sensitivity Analysis **Masalah:** Mempercayai satu set asumsi (σ, DEFF, response rate) tanpa menguji bagaimana hasil berubah jika asumsi dilanggar. ## Template R Script: Full Workflow ::: {.panel-tabset} ### R ```{r} #| label: full-workflow #| code-fold: false # ============================================================ # FULL SURVEY WORKFLOW TEMPLATE # ============================================================ # --- 0. Setup --- library(dplyr) library(survey) library(srvyr) library(ggplot2) # --- 1. Load Population / Frame --- pop <- load_population() N <- nrow(pop) # --- 2. Design Parameters --- config <- list( n_target = 300, # target sample size n_psu = 15, # number of PSUs (districts) m_per_psu = 20, # SSU per PSU strat_var = "region", # stratification variable psu_var = "district", # PSU identifier target_vars = c("income", "expenditure", "satisfaction"), deff_assumed = 2.0, response_rate = 0.75 ) # Adjusted sample size n_adjusted <- ceiling(config$n_target * config$deff_assumed / config$response_rate) # --- 3. Sample Selection --- set.seed(2024) # Stage 1: Select PSUs (stratified PPS) district_info <- pop |> summarise(N_d = n(), .by = district) |> mutate(prob_d = N_d / sum(N_d)) selected_psus <- pop |> distinct(district, region) |> left_join(district_info, by = "district") |> group_by(region) |> group_modify(~ slice_sample(.x, n = min(config$n_psu, nrow(.x)), weight_by = N_d)) |> ungroup() |> pull(district) # Stage 2: SRS within PSUs survey_sample <- pop |> filter(district %in% selected_psus) |> group_by(district) |> group_modify(~ slice_sample(.x, n = min(config$m_per_psu, nrow(.x)))) |> ungroup() # --- 4. Compute Weights --- survey_sample <- survey_sample |> left_join(district_info, by = "district") |> mutate( pi_1 = length(selected_psus) * prob_d, # PSU selection prob pi_2 = config$m_per_psu / N_d, # SSU selection prob base_weight = 1 / (pi_1 * pi_2) ) # --- 5. Setup Survey Design --- des <- svydesign( id = ~district, strata = ~region, weights = ~base_weight, data = survey_sample, nest = TRUE ) # --- 6. Calibration (Raking) --- pop_urban <- xtabs(~urban_rural, data = pop) pop_region <- xtabs(~region, data = pop) des_cal <- rake( des, sample.margins = list(~urban_rural, ~region), population.margins = list(pop_urban, pop_region) ) # --- 7. Analysis --- # Overall means overall <- svymean(~income + expenditure, des_cal) # By subgroup by_region <- svyby(~income, ~region, des_cal, svymean) by_urban <- svyby(~income, ~urban_rural, des_cal, svymean) # Regression model <- svyglm(income ~ urban_rural + education + household_size, design = des_cal) # --- 8. Diagnostics --- final_wts <- weights(des_cal) wt_diagnostics <- tibble( n_actual = nrow(survey_sample), n_eff = sum(final_wts)^2 / sum(final_wts^2), cv_weights = sd(final_wts) / mean(final_wts), deff_w = 1 + (sd(final_wts) / mean(final_wts))^2, sum_weights = sum(final_wts), ratio_max_min = max(final_wts) / min(final_wts) ) # --- 9. Results Summary --- results_summary <- tibble( Variable = c("Income", "Expenditure"), Estimate = format(round(coef(overall)), big.mark = ","), SE = format(round(SE(overall)), big.mark = ","), CI_Lower = format(round(confint(overall)[, 1]), big.mark = ","), CI_Upper = format(round(confint(overall)[, 2]), big.mark = ",") ) results_summary |> gt() |> tab_header( title = "Survey Results Summary", subtitle = sprintf("n = %d | n_eff = %.0f | CV(w) = %.3f", nrow(survey_sample), wt_diagnostics$n_eff, wt_diagnostics$cv_weights) ) ``` ### Python ```{python} #| label: full-workflow-py #| code-fold: false # ============================================================ # FULL SURVEY WORKFLOW TEMPLATE (Python) # ============================================================ import numpy as np import pandas as pd from scipy.stats import norm np.random.seed(2024) # --- 1. Load Data --- pop_py = r.pop.copy() N = len(pop_py) # --- 2. Two-stage Sampling --- n_psu = 15 m_per_psu = 20 district_info = pop_py.groupby('district').size().reset_index(name='N_d') district_info['prob'] = district_info['N_d'] / district_info['N_d'].sum() # PPS selection selected = district_info.sample(n=n_psu, weights='prob', random_state=2024)['district'].values # SRS within PSU samples = [] for d in selected: d_data = pop_py[pop_py['district'] == d] s = d_data.sample(n=min(m_per_psu, len(d_data)), random_state=2024) d_info = district_info[district_info['district'] == d].iloc[0] s = s.copy() s['pi_1'] = n_psu * d_info['prob'] s['pi_2'] = m_per_psu / d_info['N_d'] s['weight'] = 1 / (s['pi_1'] * s['pi_2']) samples.append(s) survey_sample = pd.concat(samples) # --- 3. Weighted Analysis --- weighted_mean = np.average(survey_sample['income'], weights=survey_sample['weight']) true_mean = pop_py['income'].mean() # n_eff w = survey_sample['weight'].values n_eff = w.sum()**2 / (w**2).sum() cv_w = w.std() / w.mean() # By region by_region = survey_sample.groupby('region').apply( lambda g: pd.Series({ 'weighted_mean': np.average(g['income'], weights=g['weight']), 'n': len(g) }) ).reset_index() results = pd.DataFrame({ 'Metric': ['Weighted Mean Income', 'True Mean', 'Bias', 'n', 'n_eff', 'CV(weights)'], 'Value': [f'{weighted_mean:,.0f}', f'{true_mean:,.0f}', f'{weighted_mean - true_mean:,.0f}', str(len(survey_sample)), f'{n_eff:.1f}', f'{cv_w:.4f}'] }) results ``` ::: ## Case Study: Survei Kepuasan Pelanggan Bank ### Konteks Sebuah bank dengan **50.000 nasabah aktif** di **10 kota** ingin mengukur kepuasan pelanggan (skor 1–5). Tujuan: 1. Estimasi overall mean kepuasan dengan MoE ≤ 0.15 (95% CI) 2. Estimasi per kota (10 kota) dengan MoE ≤ 0.30 3. Perbandingan kepuasan nasabah digital vs conventional 4. Identifikasi faktor-faktor yang mempengaruhi kepuasan ### Step 1: Penentuan Desain ```{mermaid} %%| fig-width: 8 %%| label: fig-case-design flowchart LR A["Frame: Database
50K nasabah"] --> B["Strata: 10 kota ×
2 tipe (digital/conv)"] B --> C["Alokasi: Proportional
dengan minimum per cell"] C --> D["Selection: SRS
within strata"] D --> E["Adjustment:
NR + calibration"] ``` ### Step 2: Perhitungan Sample Size ::: {.panel-tabset} #### R ```{r} #| label: case-sample-size # Parameters N_bank <- 50000 moe_overall <- 0.15 moe_city <- 0.30 alpha <- 0.05 z <- qnorm(1 - alpha/2) sigma_est <- 1.0 # estimated SD of satisfaction (1-5 scale) deff <- 1.5 # within-city clustering mild response_rate <- 0.65 # expected # Overall sample size n_overall <- ceiling(z^2 * sigma_est^2 / moe_overall^2) n_overall_fpc <- ceiling(n_overall / (1 + n_overall / N_bank)) # Per city (10 cities, each needs n_city) n_per_city <- ceiling(z^2 * sigma_est^2 / moe_city^2) n_cities_total <- n_per_city * 10 # Take maximum n_srs <- max(n_overall_fpc, n_cities_total) # Adjust n_adjusted <- ceiling(n_srs * deff / response_rate) tibble( Parameter = c("n (overall MoE)", "n (per-city MoE × 10)", "n (SRS, take max)", "DEFF", "Response rate", "n (final adjusted)"), Value = c(n_overall_fpc, n_cities_total, n_srs, deff, response_rate, n_adjusted) ) |> mutate(Value = format(round(Value), big.mark = ",")) |> gt() |> tab_header( title = "Case Study: Sample Size Calculation", subtitle = "Bank Customer Satisfaction Survey" ) ``` #### Python ```{python} #| label: case-sample-size-py import numpy as np from scipy.stats import norm import pandas as pd N_bank = 50000 z = norm.ppf(0.975) sigma = 1.0 deff = 1.5 rr = 0.65 n_overall = np.ceil(z**2 * sigma**2 / 0.15**2) n_overall_fpc = np.ceil(n_overall / (1 + n_overall / N_bank)) n_per_city = np.ceil(z**2 * sigma**2 / 0.30**2) n_cities = n_per_city * 10 n_srs = max(n_overall_fpc, n_cities) n_final = int(np.ceil(n_srs * deff / rr)) pd.DataFrame({ 'Parameter': ['n (overall)', 'n (10 cities)', 'n (SRS max)', 'n (final adjusted)'], 'Value': [int(n_overall_fpc), int(n_cities), int(n_srs), n_final] }) ``` ::: ### Step 3: Sampling, Weighting, dan Estimasi ::: {.panel-tabset} #### R ```{r} #| label: case-full-analysis #| fig-cap: "Case study: estimasi kepuasan per kota dengan 95% CI." #| fig-width: 10 #| fig-height: 6 set.seed(2024) # Simulate bank customer population bank_pop <- tibble( customer_id = 1:N_bank, city = sample(paste0("Kota_", sprintf("%02d", 1:10)), N_bank, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.20, 0.15, 0.12, 0.10, 0.10, 0.08, 0.08, 0.07, 0.05, 0.05)), type = sample(c("Digital", "Conventional"), N_bank, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.45, 0.55)), tenure_years = round(runif(N_bank, 0.5, 15), 1), transaction_freq = rpois(N_bank, lambda = ifelse( sample(c("Digital", "Conventional"), N_bank, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.45, 0.55)) == "Digital", 12, 5 )) ) # Satisfaction correlated with type, city, tenure city_effect <- setNames(rnorm(10, 0, 0.3), paste0("Kota_", sprintf("%02d", 1:10))) bank_pop <- bank_pop |> mutate( satisfaction = pmin(pmax(round( 3.2 + 0.3 * (type == "Digital") + city_effect[city] + 0.02 * tenure_years + rnorm(N_bank, 0, 0.8) ), 1), 5) ) # Stratified sampling n_final <- n_adjusted bank_pop <- bank_pop |> mutate(stratum = paste(city, type, sep = "_")) strata_counts <- bank_pop |> count(stratum, name = "N_h") # Proportional allocation with minimum 15 per stratum allocation <- strata_counts |> mutate( W_h = N_h / N_bank, n_h_prop = pmax(round(n_final * W_h), 15) ) # Draw sample bank_sample <- bank_pop |> left_join(allocation |> select(stratum, n_h_prop), by = "stratum") |> group_by(stratum, n_h_prop) |> group_modify(~ slice_sample(.x, n = min(.y$n_h_prop[[1]], nrow(.x)))) |> ungroup() |> select(-n_h_prop) # Simulate non-response bank_sample <- bank_sample |> mutate( responded = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.65 + 0.05 * (type == "Digital")) ) bank_respondents <- bank_sample |> filter(responded == 1) # Compute weights bank_respondents <- bank_respondents |> left_join(strata_counts, by = "stratum") |> mutate( n_h_resp = n(), .by = stratum ) |> mutate( base_weight = N_h / n_h_resp ) # Survey design des_bank <- svydesign( id = ~1, strata = ~stratum, weights = ~base_weight, data = bank_respondents ) # Calibrate to known city distribution pop_city_tbl <- xtabs(~city, data = bank_pop) des_bank_cal <- postStratify(des_bank, ~city, pop_city_tbl) # Estimates overall_sat <- svymean(~satisfaction, des_bank_cal) by_city <- svyby(~satisfaction, ~city, des_bank_cal, svymean) by_type <- svyby(~satisfaction, ~type, des_bank_cal, svymean) # Plot by_city_df <- as.data.frame(by_city) |> as_tibble() |> mutate( lower = satisfaction - 1.96 * se, upper = satisfaction + 1.96 * se ) ggplot(by_city_df, aes(x = reorder(city, satisfaction), y = satisfaction)) + geom_point(size = 3, color = "#2563eb") + geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.2, color = "#2563eb") + geom_hline(yintercept = coef(overall_sat), linetype = "dashed", color = "#dc2626") + coord_flip() + labs( title = "Kepuasan Pelanggan per Kota (95% CI)", subtitle = sprintf("Overall mean = %.2f (SE = %.3f) | n respondents = %d", coef(overall_sat), SE(overall_sat), nrow(bank_respondents)), x = NULL, y = "Mean Satisfaction (1-5)" ) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(2.5, 4.5)) ``` ```{r} #| label: tbl-case-results #| tbl-cap: "Hasil survei: estimasi kepuasan per tipe nasabah." by_type |> as.data.frame() |> as_tibble() |> mutate( CI = sprintf("[%.2f, %.2f]", satisfaction - 1.96 * se, satisfaction + 1.96 * se) ) |> select(type, satisfaction, se, CI) |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "Satisfaction by Customer Type") |> cols_label(type = "Type", satisfaction = "Mean", se = "SE", CI = "95% CI") |> fmt_number(columns = c(satisfaction, se), decimals = 3) ``` ```{r} #| label: case-regression # Factors affecting satisfaction model_bank <- svyglm(satisfaction ~ type + tenure_years + transaction_freq, design = des_bank_cal) broom::tidy(model_bank, conf.int = TRUE) |> mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 3))) |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "Regression: Faktor yang Mempengaruhi Kepuasan") ``` #### Python ```{python} #| label: case-analysis-py import numpy as np import pandas as pd bank_resp = r.bank_respondents.copy() weights = bank_resp['base_weight'].values satisfaction = bank_resp['satisfaction'].values # Overall weighted mean overall_mean = np.average(satisfaction, weights=weights) # By type by_type = bank_resp.groupby('type').apply( lambda g: pd.Series({ 'weighted_mean': np.average(g['satisfaction'], weights=g['base_weight']), 'n': len(g) }) ).reset_index() # By city by_city = bank_resp.groupby('city').apply( lambda g: pd.Series({ 'weighted_mean': np.average(g['satisfaction'], weights=g['base_weight']), 'n': len(g) }) ).reset_index().sort_values('weighted_mean', ascending=False) results = pd.concat([ pd.DataFrame({'Metric': ['Overall Mean', 'n respondents', 'n_eff'], 'Value': [f'{overall_mean:.3f}', str(len(bank_resp)), f'{weights.sum()**2 / (weights**2).sum():.0f}']}), ]) results ``` ::: ### Step 4: Quality Check & Reporting ```{r} #| label: case-diagnostics #| fig-cap: "Case study diagnostics: distribusi weight dan marginal check." #| fig-width: 10 #| fig-height: 5 library(patchwork) final_wts_bank <- weights(des_bank_cal) p1 <- ggplot(tibble(w = final_wts_bank), aes(x = w)) + geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "#2563eb", alpha = 0.7) + labs(title = "Weight Distribution", x = "Weight", y = "Count") # Marginal check: city pop_city_prop <- prop.table(table(bank_pop$city)) samp_city_prop <- prop.table(svytable(~city, des_bank_cal)) city_check <- tibble( city = rep(names(pop_city_prop), 2), proportion = c(pop_city_prop, samp_city_prop), source = rep(c("Population", "Weighted Sample"), each = length(pop_city_prop)) ) p2 <- ggplot(city_check, aes(x = city, y = proportion, fill = source)) + geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.8) + scale_fill_manual(values = c("Population" = "#059669", "Weighted Sample" = "#2563eb")) + labs(title = "Marginal Check: City Distribution", x = NULL, y = "Proportion", fill = NULL) + scale_y_continuous(labels = percent) + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) p1 | p2 ``` ### Summary: Langkah-langkah Utama Case Study | Step | Action | Output | |------|--------|--------| | 1 | Define objectives & precision requirements | MoE per tujuan | | 2 | Evaluate frame (database nasabah) | Coverage assessment | | 3 | Choose design (stratified by city × type) | Design specification | | 4 | Calculate sample size (multi-objective + DEFF + NR) | n = `r n_adjusted` | | 5 | Allocate to strata (proportional, min 15/stratum) | Allocation table | | 6 | Select sample & conduct survey | Raw data | | 7 | Compute base weights (1/π_i) | Weighted dataset | | 8 | Adjust for non-response (weighting class) | NR-adjusted weights | | 9 | Calibrate to known totals (city distribution) | Final weights | | 10 | Analyze with survey design (svydesign + svymean etc.) | Estimates + SE + CI | | 11 | Diagnostics (weight distribution, marginals) | QC report | | 12 | Report (estimates, methodology, limitations) | Final deliverable | ::: {.takeaways} ### Key Takeaways 1. **Gunakan decision flowchart** untuk memilih metode — jangan default ke convenience sampling hanya karena mudah. 2. **Checklist sebelum sampling** mencegah kesalahan yang mahal — terutama frame quality, DEFF, dan non-response rate. 3. **10 kesalahan terbanyak** semuanya bisa dihindari dengan perencanaan yang matang dan pemahaman design-based inference. 4. **Full workflow template** menyediakan kerangka kerja yang reproducible — dari sampling frame hingga estimasi akhir. 5. **Case study menunjukkan** bahwa setiap langkah mempengaruhi kualitas hasil — short-cut di satu langkah memperbesar error di hilir. ::: ::: {.exercises} ### Exercises 1. **Decision framework.** Anda diminta mendesain survei pengeluaran rumah tangga di provinsi Jawa Tengah. Frame tersedia: daftar blok sensus SP2020 (3,5 juta RT, 8.500 desa/kelurahan). Budget: 200 juta. Estimasi level kabupaten (35 kab/kota). Gunakan flowchart untuk menentukan desain, lalu hitung sample size. 2. **Error diagnosis.** Diberikan dataset hasil survei (tanpa weight, tanpa design specification). Atasan meminta Anda menganalisis data ini. Tulis memo singkat yang menjelaskan: (a) risiko menganalisis tanpa weight, (b) informasi apa yang perlu Anda minta ke tim lapangan, (c) minimum correction yang bisa dilakukan. 3. **Case study extension.** Modifikasi case study bank di atas: tambahkan analisis per segmen usia (<30, 30-50, >50). Hitung apakah sampel cukup untuk estimasi per segmen. Jika tidak, berapa tambahan sampel yang diperlukan? ::: ### Further Reading - @lohr2021, Chapter 14: Practical guidelines for survey design. - @groves2009, Chapter 4: Coverage and sampling frame issues. - @lumley2010, Chapter 1-3: Complete workflow analysis di R.
markdown.srcMarkdownNoYaml:
```{r} #| label: setup #| include: false source("_common.R") pop <- load_population() N <- nrow(pop) ``` Bab ini menyatukan seluruh materi menjadi panduan praktis yang dapat langsung diterapkan di lapangan. ## Decision Flowchart Gunakan flowchart berikut untuk menentukan metode sampling yang tepat: ```{mermaid} %%| fig-width: 10 %%| label: fig-decision-flowchart flowchart TD START["🎯 Tujuan: Estimasi parameter
populasi?"] -->|Ya| FRAME{"Sampling frame
tersedia?"} START -->|Tidak, hanya eksplorasi| NP["Non-probability
sampling OK"] FRAME -->|Ya, lengkap| BUDGET{"Budget &
logistik OK
untuk SRS?"} FRAME -->|Tidak lengkap| HIDDEN{"Hidden /
hard-to-reach
population?"} FRAME -->|Parsial| ADJUST["Perbaiki frame +
model-based
adjustment"] HIDDEN -->|Ya| SNOW["Snowball / RDS
+ network-size
weighting"] HIDDEN -->|Tidak| QUOTA["Quota sampling +
calibration
weighting"] BUDGET -->|Ya| HETERO{"Populasi
heterogen
antar group?"} BUDGET -->|Tidak, terlalu mahal| GEO{"Constraint
geografis?"} HETERO -->|Ya, stratifying var ada| STRAT["✅ Stratified
Random Sampling"] HETERO -->|Tidak, cukup homogen| SRS["✅ Simple Random
Sampling"] GEO -->|Ya| CLUSTER{"Cluster size
bervariasi?"} GEO -->|Tidak| STRAT CLUSTER -->|Ya, sangat bervariasi| PPS["✅ PPS Cluster
Sampling"] CLUSTER -->|Tidak, relatif sama| CLUST_SRS["✅ Cluster +
SRS dalam cluster"] STRAT --> SUBGROUP{"Perlu analisis
subgroup?"} SRS --> SUBGROUP PPS --> SUBGROUP CLUST_SRS --> SUBGROUP SUBGROUP -->|Ya| INFLATE["Inflate n per
subgroup +
DEFF adjustment"] SUBGROUP -->|Tidak| CALC["Hitung n
(Chapter 5)"] INFLATE --> CALC style START fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb,stroke-width:2px style STRAT fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669,stroke-width:2px style SRS fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669,stroke-width:2px style PPS fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669,stroke-width:2px style CLUST_SRS fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669,stroke-width:2px style NP fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706,stroke-width:2px style SNOW fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706,stroke-width:2px style QUOTA fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706,stroke-width:2px ``` ## Checklist Sebelum Sampling ::: {.callout-tip} ## Pre-Sampling Checklist ### 1. Sampling Frame - [ ] Apakah frame tersedia dan up-to-date? - [ ] Berapa coverage rate terhadap target population? - [ ] Apakah ada duplikasi, undercoverage, atau overcoverage? - [ ] Kapan terakhir frame diupdate? ### 2. Stratifikasi - [ ] Variabel stratifikasi apa yang tersedia di frame? - [ ] Apakah variabel tersebut berkorelasi dengan variabel survei utama? - [ ] Berapa strata optimal? (jangan terlalu banyak — minimum n per stratum ≥ 20) ### 3. Budget & Logistik - [ ] Berapa total budget untuk fieldwork? - [ ] Berapa cost per interview (termasuk transportasi, honorarium, supervisi)? - [ ] Berapa banyak interviewer tersedia dan terlatih? - [ ] Berapa lama periode fieldwork? ### 4. Subgroup Analysis - [ ] Subgroup mana yang harus dianalisis secara terpisah? - [ ] Apakah perlu estimasi per subgroup atau hanya overall? - [ ] Berapa presisi minimum per subgroup? ### 5. Non-Response - [ ] Berapa expected response rate (dari survei sebelumnya)? - [ ] Apakah ada strategi follow-up untuk non-respondent? - [ ] Data apa yang tersedia untuk non-response adjustment? ### 6. Quality Control - [ ] Bagaimana verifikasi data di lapangan? - [ ] Berapa persen back-checking yang dilakukan? - [ ] Apakah ada real-time monitoring dashboard? ::: ## 10 Kesalahan Paling Sering ### 1. Mengabaikan Design Effect ::: {.callout-important} **Masalah:** Menghitung sample size dengan formula SRS, lalu menggunakan cluster sampling di lapangan. **Dampak:** Presisi aktual jauh lebih rendah dari yang diharapkan. Jika DEFF = 3, presisi Anda setara dengan n/3. **Solusi:** Selalu kalikan n_SRS dengan DEFF. Jika ICC tidak diketahui, gunakan DEFF = 2.0 sebagai default konservatif. ::: ### 2. Mengabaikan Non-Response **Masalah:** Menargetkan n = 400 tapi response rate hanya 60% → effective n = 240. **Solusi:** $n_{final} = n_{target} / (1 - r)$. Budget untuk follow-up. ### 3. Menganalisis Tanpa Weight **Masalah:** Menggunakan `mean(data$y)` alih-alih `svymean(~y, design)`. **Dampak:** Biased point estimates dan underestimated standard errors. ### 4. Melaporkan Unweighted Counts **Masalah:** "Tabel menunjukkan 45% responden tinggal di urban." Ini unweighted proportion — bukan estimasi populasi. ### 5. Subset Data Sebelum svydesign() **Masalah:** Memfilter data untuk subgroup analisis sebelum mendefinisikan survey design → SE underestimated. **Solusi:** Gunakan `subset()` pada design object. ### 6. Memperlakukan Cluster Effect Sebagai Fixed **Masalah:** Menggunakan fixed effects untuk cluster ID alih-alih memperlakukannya sebagai random effect / PSU dalam survey design. ### 7. Tidak Memeriksa Kualitas Frame **Masalah:** Langsung sampling dari frame tanpa assessment → coverage error tidak terdeteksi. ### 8. Over-Stratification **Masalah:** Terlalu banyak strata → beberapa strata memiliki n < 10 → estimasi tidak stabil. **Rule of thumb:** Minimum 2 PSU per stratum untuk estimasi varians. ### 9. Mengabaikan Multi-Objective **Masalah:** Menghitung n hanya untuk satu variabel, padahal survei mengukur 20 variabel → beberapa variabel underpowered. ### 10. Tidak Melakukan Sensitivity Analysis **Masalah:** Mempercayai satu set asumsi (σ, DEFF, response rate) tanpa menguji bagaimana hasil berubah jika asumsi dilanggar. ## Template R Script: Full Workflow ::: {.panel-tabset} ### R ```{r} #| label: full-workflow #| code-fold: false # ============================================================ # FULL SURVEY WORKFLOW TEMPLATE # ============================================================ # --- 0. Setup --- library(dplyr) library(survey) library(srvyr) library(ggplot2) # --- 1. Load Population / Frame --- pop <- load_population() N <- nrow(pop) # --- 2. Design Parameters --- config <- list( n_target = 300, # target sample size n_psu = 15, # number of PSUs (districts) m_per_psu = 20, # SSU per PSU strat_var = "region", # stratification variable psu_var = "district", # PSU identifier target_vars = c("income", "expenditure", "satisfaction"), deff_assumed = 2.0, response_rate = 0.75 ) # Adjusted sample size n_adjusted <- ceiling(config$n_target * config$deff_assumed / config$response_rate) # --- 3. Sample Selection --- set.seed(2024) # Stage 1: Select PSUs (stratified PPS) district_info <- pop |> summarise(N_d = n(), .by = district) |> mutate(prob_d = N_d / sum(N_d)) selected_psus <- pop |> distinct(district, region) |> left_join(district_info, by = "district") |> group_by(region) |> group_modify(~ slice_sample(.x, n = min(config$n_psu, nrow(.x)), weight_by = N_d)) |> ungroup() |> pull(district) # Stage 2: SRS within PSUs survey_sample <- pop |> filter(district %in% selected_psus) |> group_by(district) |> group_modify(~ slice_sample(.x, n = min(config$m_per_psu, nrow(.x)))) |> ungroup() # --- 4. Compute Weights --- survey_sample <- survey_sample |> left_join(district_info, by = "district") |> mutate( pi_1 = length(selected_psus) * prob_d, # PSU selection prob pi_2 = config$m_per_psu / N_d, # SSU selection prob base_weight = 1 / (pi_1 * pi_2) ) # --- 5. Setup Survey Design --- des <- svydesign( id = ~district, strata = ~region, weights = ~base_weight, data = survey_sample, nest = TRUE ) # --- 6. Calibration (Raking) --- pop_urban <- xtabs(~urban_rural, data = pop) pop_region <- xtabs(~region, data = pop) des_cal <- rake( des, sample.margins = list(~urban_rural, ~region), population.margins = list(pop_urban, pop_region) ) # --- 7. Analysis --- # Overall means overall <- svymean(~income + expenditure, des_cal) # By subgroup by_region <- svyby(~income, ~region, des_cal, svymean) by_urban <- svyby(~income, ~urban_rural, des_cal, svymean) # Regression model <- svyglm(income ~ urban_rural + education + household_size, design = des_cal) # --- 8. Diagnostics --- final_wts <- weights(des_cal) wt_diagnostics <- tibble( n_actual = nrow(survey_sample), n_eff = sum(final_wts)^2 / sum(final_wts^2), cv_weights = sd(final_wts) / mean(final_wts), deff_w = 1 + (sd(final_wts) / mean(final_wts))^2, sum_weights = sum(final_wts), ratio_max_min = max(final_wts) / min(final_wts) ) # --- 9. Results Summary --- results_summary <- tibble( Variable = c("Income", "Expenditure"), Estimate = format(round(coef(overall)), big.mark = ","), SE = format(round(SE(overall)), big.mark = ","), CI_Lower = format(round(confint(overall)[, 1]), big.mark = ","), CI_Upper = format(round(confint(overall)[, 2]), big.mark = ",") ) results_summary |> gt() |> tab_header( title = "Survey Results Summary", subtitle = sprintf("n = %d | n_eff = %.0f | CV(w) = %.3f", nrow(survey_sample), wt_diagnostics$n_eff, wt_diagnostics$cv_weights) ) ``` ### Python ```{python} #| label: full-workflow-py #| code-fold: false # ============================================================ # FULL SURVEY WORKFLOW TEMPLATE (Python) # ============================================================ import numpy as np import pandas as pd from scipy.stats import norm np.random.seed(2024) # --- 1. Load Data --- pop_py = r.pop.copy() N = len(pop_py) # --- 2. Two-stage Sampling --- n_psu = 15 m_per_psu = 20 district_info = pop_py.groupby('district').size().reset_index(name='N_d') district_info['prob'] = district_info['N_d'] / district_info['N_d'].sum() # PPS selection selected = district_info.sample(n=n_psu, weights='prob', random_state=2024)['district'].values # SRS within PSU samples = [] for d in selected: d_data = pop_py[pop_py['district'] == d] s = d_data.sample(n=min(m_per_psu, len(d_data)), random_state=2024) d_info = district_info[district_info['district'] == d].iloc[0] s = s.copy() s['pi_1'] = n_psu * d_info['prob'] s['pi_2'] = m_per_psu / d_info['N_d'] s['weight'] = 1 / (s['pi_1'] * s['pi_2']) samples.append(s) survey_sample = pd.concat(samples) # --- 3. Weighted Analysis --- weighted_mean = np.average(survey_sample['income'], weights=survey_sample['weight']) true_mean = pop_py['income'].mean() # n_eff w = survey_sample['weight'].values n_eff = w.sum()**2 / (w**2).sum() cv_w = w.std() / w.mean() # By region by_region = survey_sample.groupby('region').apply( lambda g: pd.Series({ 'weighted_mean': np.average(g['income'], weights=g['weight']), 'n': len(g) }) ).reset_index() results = pd.DataFrame({ 'Metric': ['Weighted Mean Income', 'True Mean', 'Bias', 'n', 'n_eff', 'CV(weights)'], 'Value': [f'{weighted_mean:,.0f}', f'{true_mean:,.0f}', f'{weighted_mean - true_mean:,.0f}', str(len(survey_sample)), f'{n_eff:.1f}', f'{cv_w:.4f}'] }) results ``` ::: ## Case Study: Survei Kepuasan Pelanggan Bank ### Konteks Sebuah bank dengan **50.000 nasabah aktif** di **10 kota** ingin mengukur kepuasan pelanggan (skor 1–5). Tujuan: 1. Estimasi overall mean kepuasan dengan MoE ≤ 0.15 (95% CI) 2. Estimasi per kota (10 kota) dengan MoE ≤ 0.30 3. Perbandingan kepuasan nasabah digital vs conventional 4. Identifikasi faktor-faktor yang mempengaruhi kepuasan ### Step 1: Penentuan Desain ```{mermaid} %%| fig-width: 8 %%| label: fig-case-design flowchart LR A["Frame: Database
50K nasabah"] --> B["Strata: 10 kota ×
2 tipe (digital/conv)"] B --> C["Alokasi: Proportional
dengan minimum per cell"] C --> D["Selection: SRS
within strata"] D --> E["Adjustment:
NR + calibration"] ``` ### Step 2: Perhitungan Sample Size ::: {.panel-tabset} #### R ```{r} #| label: case-sample-size # Parameters N_bank <- 50000 moe_overall <- 0.15 moe_city <- 0.30 alpha <- 0.05 z <- qnorm(1 - alpha/2) sigma_est <- 1.0 # estimated SD of satisfaction (1-5 scale) deff <- 1.5 # within-city clustering mild response_rate <- 0.65 # expected # Overall sample size n_overall <- ceiling(z^2 * sigma_est^2 / moe_overall^2) n_overall_fpc <- ceiling(n_overall / (1 + n_overall / N_bank)) # Per city (10 cities, each needs n_city) n_per_city <- ceiling(z^2 * sigma_est^2 / moe_city^2) n_cities_total <- n_per_city * 10 # Take maximum n_srs <- max(n_overall_fpc, n_cities_total) # Adjust n_adjusted <- ceiling(n_srs * deff / response_rate) tibble( Parameter = c("n (overall MoE)", "n (per-city MoE × 10)", "n (SRS, take max)", "DEFF", "Response rate", "n (final adjusted)"), Value = c(n_overall_fpc, n_cities_total, n_srs, deff, response_rate, n_adjusted) ) |> mutate(Value = format(round(Value), big.mark = ",")) |> gt() |> tab_header( title = "Case Study: Sample Size Calculation", subtitle = "Bank Customer Satisfaction Survey" ) ``` #### Python ```{python} #| label: case-sample-size-py import numpy as np from scipy.stats import norm import pandas as pd N_bank = 50000 z = norm.ppf(0.975) sigma = 1.0 deff = 1.5 rr = 0.65 n_overall = np.ceil(z**2 * sigma**2 / 0.15**2) n_overall_fpc = np.ceil(n_overall / (1 + n_overall / N_bank)) n_per_city = np.ceil(z**2 * sigma**2 / 0.30**2) n_cities = n_per_city * 10 n_srs = max(n_overall_fpc, n_cities) n_final = int(np.ceil(n_srs * deff / rr)) pd.DataFrame({ 'Parameter': ['n (overall)', 'n (10 cities)', 'n (SRS max)', 'n (final adjusted)'], 'Value': [int(n_overall_fpc), int(n_cities), int(n_srs), n_final] }) ``` ::: ### Step 3: Sampling, Weighting, dan Estimasi ::: {.panel-tabset} #### R ```{r} #| label: case-full-analysis #| fig-cap: "Case study: estimasi kepuasan per kota dengan 95% CI." #| fig-width: 10 #| fig-height: 6 set.seed(2024) # Simulate bank customer population bank_pop <- tibble( customer_id = 1:N_bank, city = sample(paste0("Kota_", sprintf("%02d", 1:10)), N_bank, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.20, 0.15, 0.12, 0.10, 0.10, 0.08, 0.08, 0.07, 0.05, 0.05)), type = sample(c("Digital", "Conventional"), N_bank, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.45, 0.55)), tenure_years = round(runif(N_bank, 0.5, 15), 1), transaction_freq = rpois(N_bank, lambda = ifelse( sample(c("Digital", "Conventional"), N_bank, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.45, 0.55)) == "Digital", 12, 5 )) ) # Satisfaction correlated with type, city, tenure city_effect <- setNames(rnorm(10, 0, 0.3), paste0("Kota_", sprintf("%02d", 1:10))) bank_pop <- bank_pop |> mutate( satisfaction = pmin(pmax(round( 3.2 + 0.3 * (type == "Digital") + city_effect[city] + 0.02 * tenure_years + rnorm(N_bank, 0, 0.8) ), 1), 5) ) # Stratified sampling n_final <- n_adjusted bank_pop <- bank_pop |> mutate(stratum = paste(city, type, sep = "_")) strata_counts <- bank_pop |> count(stratum, name = "N_h") # Proportional allocation with minimum 15 per stratum allocation <- strata_counts |> mutate( W_h = N_h / N_bank, n_h_prop = pmax(round(n_final * W_h), 15) ) # Draw sample bank_sample <- bank_pop |> left_join(allocation |> select(stratum, n_h_prop), by = "stratum") |> group_by(stratum, n_h_prop) |> group_modify(~ slice_sample(.x, n = min(.y$n_h_prop[[1]], nrow(.x)))) |> ungroup() |> select(-n_h_prop) # Simulate non-response bank_sample <- bank_sample |> mutate( responded = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.65 + 0.05 * (type == "Digital")) ) bank_respondents <- bank_sample |> filter(responded == 1) # Compute weights bank_respondents <- bank_respondents |> left_join(strata_counts, by = "stratum") |> mutate( n_h_resp = n(), .by = stratum ) |> mutate( base_weight = N_h / n_h_resp ) # Survey design des_bank <- svydesign( id = ~1, strata = ~stratum, weights = ~base_weight, data = bank_respondents ) # Calibrate to known city distribution pop_city_tbl <- xtabs(~city, data = bank_pop) des_bank_cal <- postStratify(des_bank, ~city, pop_city_tbl) # Estimates overall_sat <- svymean(~satisfaction, des_bank_cal) by_city <- svyby(~satisfaction, ~city, des_bank_cal, svymean) by_type <- svyby(~satisfaction, ~type, des_bank_cal, svymean) # Plot by_city_df <- as.data.frame(by_city) |> as_tibble() |> mutate( lower = satisfaction - 1.96 * se, upper = satisfaction + 1.96 * se ) ggplot(by_city_df, aes(x = reorder(city, satisfaction), y = satisfaction)) + geom_point(size = 3, color = "#2563eb") + geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.2, color = "#2563eb") + geom_hline(yintercept = coef(overall_sat), linetype = "dashed", color = "#dc2626") + coord_flip() + labs( title = "Kepuasan Pelanggan per Kota (95% CI)", subtitle = sprintf("Overall mean = %.2f (SE = %.3f) | n respondents = %d", coef(overall_sat), SE(overall_sat), nrow(bank_respondents)), x = NULL, y = "Mean Satisfaction (1-5)" ) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(2.5, 4.5)) ``` ```{r} #| label: tbl-case-results #| tbl-cap: "Hasil survei: estimasi kepuasan per tipe nasabah." by_type |> as.data.frame() |> as_tibble() |> mutate( CI = sprintf("[%.2f, %.2f]", satisfaction - 1.96 * se, satisfaction + 1.96 * se) ) |> select(type, satisfaction, se, CI) |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "Satisfaction by Customer Type") |> cols_label(type = "Type", satisfaction = "Mean", se = "SE", CI = "95% CI") |> fmt_number(columns = c(satisfaction, se), decimals = 3) ``` ```{r} #| label: case-regression # Factors affecting satisfaction model_bank <- svyglm(satisfaction ~ type + tenure_years + transaction_freq, design = des_bank_cal) broom::tidy(model_bank, conf.int = TRUE) |> mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 3))) |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "Regression: Faktor yang Mempengaruhi Kepuasan") ``` #### Python ```{python} #| label: case-analysis-py import numpy as np import pandas as pd bank_resp = r.bank_respondents.copy() weights = bank_resp['base_weight'].values satisfaction = bank_resp['satisfaction'].values # Overall weighted mean overall_mean = np.average(satisfaction, weights=weights) # By type by_type = bank_resp.groupby('type').apply( lambda g: pd.Series({ 'weighted_mean': np.average(g['satisfaction'], weights=g['base_weight']), 'n': len(g) }) ).reset_index() # By city by_city = bank_resp.groupby('city').apply( lambda g: pd.Series({ 'weighted_mean': np.average(g['satisfaction'], weights=g['base_weight']), 'n': len(g) }) ).reset_index().sort_values('weighted_mean', ascending=False) results = pd.concat([ pd.DataFrame({'Metric': ['Overall Mean', 'n respondents', 'n_eff'], 'Value': [f'{overall_mean:.3f}', str(len(bank_resp)), f'{weights.sum()**2 / (weights**2).sum():.0f}']}), ]) results ``` ::: ### Step 4: Quality Check & Reporting ```{r} #| label: case-diagnostics #| fig-cap: "Case study diagnostics: distribusi weight dan marginal check." #| fig-width: 10 #| fig-height: 5 library(patchwork) final_wts_bank <- weights(des_bank_cal) p1 <- ggplot(tibble(w = final_wts_bank), aes(x = w)) + geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "#2563eb", alpha = 0.7) + labs(title = "Weight Distribution", x = "Weight", y = "Count") # Marginal check: city pop_city_prop <- prop.table(table(bank_pop$city)) samp_city_prop <- prop.table(svytable(~city, des_bank_cal)) city_check <- tibble( city = rep(names(pop_city_prop), 2), proportion = c(pop_city_prop, samp_city_prop), source = rep(c("Population", "Weighted Sample"), each = length(pop_city_prop)) ) p2 <- ggplot(city_check, aes(x = city, y = proportion, fill = source)) + geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.8) + scale_fill_manual(values = c("Population" = "#059669", "Weighted Sample" = "#2563eb")) + labs(title = "Marginal Check: City Distribution", x = NULL, y = "Proportion", fill = NULL) + scale_y_continuous(labels = percent) + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) p1 | p2 ``` ### Summary: Langkah-langkah Utama Case Study | Step | Action | Output | |------|--------|--------| | 1 | Define objectives & precision requirements | MoE per tujuan | | 2 | Evaluate frame (database nasabah) | Coverage assessment | | 3 | Choose design (stratified by city × type) | Design specification | | 4 | Calculate sample size (multi-objective + DEFF + NR) | n = `r n_adjusted` | | 5 | Allocate to strata (proportional, min 15/stratum) | Allocation table | | 6 | Select sample & conduct survey | Raw data | | 7 | Compute base weights (1/π_i) | Weighted dataset | | 8 | Adjust for non-response (weighting class) | NR-adjusted weights | | 9 | Calibrate to known totals (city distribution) | Final weights | | 10 | Analyze with survey design (svydesign + svymean etc.) | Estimates + SE + CI | | 11 | Diagnostics (weight distribution, marginals) | QC report | | 12 | Report (estimates, methodology, limitations) | Final deliverable | ::: {.takeaways} ### Key Takeaways 1. **Gunakan decision flowchart** untuk memilih metode — jangan default ke convenience sampling hanya karena mudah. 2. **Checklist sebelum sampling** mencegah kesalahan yang mahal — terutama frame quality, DEFF, dan non-response rate. 3. **10 kesalahan terbanyak** semuanya bisa dihindari dengan perencanaan yang matang dan pemahaman design-based inference. 4. **Full workflow template** menyediakan kerangka kerja yang reproducible — dari sampling frame hingga estimasi akhir. 5. **Case study menunjukkan** bahwa setiap langkah mempengaruhi kualitas hasil — short-cut di satu langkah memperbesar error di hilir. ::: ::: {.exercises} ### Exercises 1. **Decision framework.** Anda diminta mendesain survei pengeluaran rumah tangga di provinsi Jawa Tengah. Frame tersedia: daftar blok sensus SP2020 (3,5 juta RT, 8.500 desa/kelurahan). Budget: 200 juta. Estimasi level kabupaten (35 kab/kota). Gunakan flowchart untuk menentukan desain, lalu hitung sample size. 2. **Error diagnosis.** Diberikan dataset hasil survei (tanpa weight, tanpa design specification). Atasan meminta Anda menganalisis data ini. Tulis memo singkat yang menjelaskan: (a) risiko menganalisis tanpa weight, (b) informasi apa yang perlu Anda minta ke tim lapangan, (c) minimum correction yang bisa dilakukan. 3. **Case study extension.** Modifikasi case study bank di atas: tambahkan analisis per segmen usia (<30, 30-50, >50). Hitung apakah sampel cukup untuk estimasi per segmen. Jika tidak, berapa tambahan sampel yang diperlukan? ::: ### Further Reading - @lohr2021, Chapter 14: Practical guidelines for survey design. - @groves2009, Chapter 4: Coverage and sampling frame issues. - @lumley2010, Chapter 1-3: Complete workflow analysis di R.

language.appendix-attribution-cite-as: Untuk atribusi, mohon kutip karya ini sebagai:

metadata.title: Practical Guide & Decision Framework
metadata.subtitle: Decision flowchart, checklist, common mistakes, dan case study end-to-end
